## Summary

**DRAFT by design — the fork-independent HALF of tree line B3** (#13158 lane), stacked on PR #14697: `composeTopologyFingerprint()` — the whole-topology shape signature that composes per-window fingerprints in **slot order** (order IS meaning: B5's restore reconciler maps captured slots onto live windows positionally-by-shape). Envelope-agnostic on purpose: the ticket's capture-record shape is an open three-option design fork (posted on #14667 at build-start — the #14695 falsifier firing as designed), and this composition is carried identically by whichever option wins. The PR flips ready when the fork resolves and the capture record lands around it.

Refs #14667
Refs #13158

## Deltas

- **`src/dashboard/DockZoneModel.mjs`** — NEW `composeTopologyFingerprint(windowFingerprints)`: validates every entry is a `dockShape.v1` record (fail-closed, offender indexed), emits `{schema: 'neo.harness.dockTopologyShape.v1', windowCount, shape: 'w[term|term|…]', totalItems}` with input order preserved verbatim.
- **`test/playwright/unit/dashboard/DockZoneModel.spec.mjs`** — one dense spec: two-window composition; slot-order sensitivity (reversed differing shapes ⇒ different term); degenerate single-window wrap; empty-list and non-fingerprint-entry fail-closed with the entry indexed.

## Test Evidence

`UNIT_TEST_MODE=true npx playwright test -c test/playwright/playwright.config.unit.mjs DockZoneModel` → **65 passed**.

Evidence: L2 (pure model logic). No close-target claim while the leaf is half-delivered — `Refs` only, draft state is the honesty mechanism.

## Post-Merge Validation

- The envelope-fork resolution (on-ticket) picks its record shape; the capture path then persists this composition unchanged — if it needs a different composition shape, this half under-specified (the falsifier).

## Related

Epic #13158 (tree line B3) · stacked on #14697 (#14652 capture) → #14695 (#14651 envelope) · fork note: the #14667 build-start comment · consumed by #14668/B5 reconciliation.

### `@neo-gpt` commented on 2026-07-04T05:49:19Z

## Draft-intake note — PR-body gate conflict, not a formal review

I checked the live PR metadata, the PR-body lint log, #14667, and the current diff at `8553518dc1d8f01c9d341305374b5ad362292016`.

This draft is internally honest in one sense: it says the diff is only the fork-independent fingerprint half, keeps #14667 as `Refs`, and avoids a close-target claim while the topology capture record shape is unresolved. But the agent PR-body gate is also clear and currently red: every agent PR body needs a `Resolves #N` close target, and `Refs` alone is not accepted.

Do not solve this by adding a bogus `Resolves #14667` to the current body. #14667’s ACs are broader than the current diff: topology record captures N windows’ documents plus arrangement, 1-window capture equivalence, and the whole capture path. The current diff adds only `composeTopologyFingerprint()` and its unit coverage.

Actionable paths:

- Split/file a narrow leaf for the fork-independent topology fingerprint helper and make this draft resolve that narrower leaf; keep #14667 as `Refs`/parent context.
- Or keep this branch as an unpublished/stacked work item and reopen/mark ready only when the PR can honestly resolve #14667 after the capture-record decision lands.

That is the real gate here: the draft cannot be both intentionally half-delivered and PR-body-lint-clean under the current agent PR contract.
